L1.area: Container build execution and filesystem management

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in a container build system where the cleanup logic that removes temporary placeholder files created for mount points can be tricked into resolving paths outside the container's root directory. A malicious build frontend or build definition can exploit this to achieve arbitrary file deletion on the host filesystem.

L3.area: Container executor mount point stub creation and cleanup
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists in the cleanup routine that removes temporary placeholder files created for RUN --mount directives during container builds. After the container finishes executing, the routine does not re-verify that the resolved paths of these placeholder files still reside within the container root, allowing a malicious build definition to use symlinks to redirect the cleanup to delete arbitrary files on the host system.

ground_truth.reason: The mount stub cleanup routine does not validate that resolved paths remain within the container root after execution, allowing a malicious build frontend to craft symlinks that cause the cleaner to delete arbitrary files on the host filesystem.
